response:
## Testing Whether 389 is Prime

### Step 1: Find the square root of 389
√389 ≈ 19.7

So I only need to test prime divisors **up to 19**.

### Step 2: Test each prime divisor

**Primes to test: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19**

| Divisor | Test | Result |
|---------|------|--------|
| 2 | 389 is odd | ✗ Not divisible |
| 3 | 3+8+9 = 20, not divisible by 3 | ✗ Not divisible |
| 5 | Doesn't end in 0 or 5 | ✗ Not divisible |
| 7 | 389 ÷ 7 = 55.57... | ✗ Not divisible |
| 11 | 389 ÷ 11 = 35.36... | ✗ Not divisible |
| 13 | 389 ÷ 13 = 29.92... | ✗ Not divisible |
| 17 | 389 ÷ 17 = 22.88... | ✗ Not divisible |
| 19 | 389 ÷ 19 = 20.47... | ✗ Not divisible |

### Conclusion

Since 389 is not divisible by any prime up to its square root, **389 is prime**. ✓
